## Overview

What this feature does, who uses it, and why it exists.

## User Flows

Main user scenarios and role-specific differences.

## Routes And UI

Pages, layouts, navigation entrypoints, loading states, empty states, and errors.

## APIs And Server Actions

Endpoints, methods, auth classification, request/response shape, and errors.

## Data Model

Tables, key columns, relationships, RPCs, and storage buckets.

## Access Control

Feature-specific permissions/scopes, page guards, API guards, RLS/RPC notes, and service-role exceptions.

## State And Lifecycle

Statuses, transitions, background/provider events, and user state.

## Integrations

External providers, Supabase, notifications, payments, email, or storage.

## Tests

Unit, route/API, integration, e2e, smoke, and known coverage gaps.

## Operations

Debugging notes, common failures, logs, and support/admin actions.

## Known Limitations

Intentional exceptions, deferred cleanup, risks, and future work.
